name: GOLFTEC Woodland Hills
about: GOLFTEC Woodland Hills is a high-tech indoor golf training facility focused on data-driven swing instruction, club fitting, and practice. The venue utilizes advanced technology like OPTIMOTION motion capture, high-speed video analysis, and launch monitors to provide detailed feedback on ball flight, club path, and impact conditions. Coaching is central to the experience, with personalized game plans and PGA award-winning instructors guiding players of all skill levels through improvement strategies. While a pro shop with a 10% discount on gear is available, the primary focus is structured learning rather than social simulation or entertainment.
why_golfers_like_it: Data-driven instruction using advanced motion tracking technology like OPTIMOTION and launch monitors
Personalized coaching with PGA award-winning instructors to create customized game plans
Comprehensive club fitting services that claim to add 21 more yards on average

food_and_drink.notes: No mention of an on-site kitchen or food service.
No mention of alcohol service; appears to be a focused training environment.

simulator_hardware.notes: Mentioned as a product provided by the company and used in their services, likely integrated into their launch monitor systems.

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own clubs?
faq.answer: While you can bring your own clubs, GOLFTEC also offers club rentals and will provide all necessary equipment for lessons and fittings.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drinks?
faq.answer: There is no mention of a food and beverage service at this location, and it appears to be focused on training rather than a social venue. Outside food and drinks are likely not permitted.

faq.question: How long does it take to play a full 18 holes?
faq.answer: GOLFTEC is not designed for traditional 18-hole play. The focus is on lessons and practice sessions, which are booked on a per-bay, per-hour basis.

faq.question: Is this facility kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, GOLFTEC offers programs for juniors and youth with advanced technology like video analysis and launch monitors designed to help young golfers understand their swing and progress.
